title: Foundations of AI & Large Language Models
description: A standalone introductory course — No coding required. Understand what AI is, how LLMs work, and the landscape of models before writing a single line of code.
longDescription:
A standalone introductory course — No coding required. Understand what AI is, how LLMs work, and the landscape of models before writing a single line of code.

**Goal:** Build a solid conceptual understanding of AI, LLMs, and the ecosystem

modules.title: What is Artificial Intelligence
What are Large Language Models (LLMs)

lessons.title: From rule-based NLP to neural language models
Compare a base model vs a chat model

modules.title: How LLMs Work — Transformers, Tokens, Attention
Key Concepts Every AI Practitioner Must Know
The LLM Landscape — Who's Who

lessons.title: Compare responses from 5 different LLMs on the same prompt

modules.title: Closed-Source vs Open-Source Models

lessons.title: Deploy the same task on a closed-source model (Claude/GPT) and an open model (Llama via Ollama)

modules.title: Understanding AI Safety and Ethics
The AI Development Stack

lessons.title: What you'll build in this program

title: Python for AI — Quick Refresher
description: A fast-paced refresher course — Get up to speed with the Python skills you need for AI development. Covers core syntax, API interaction, JSON handling, async programming, and environment management.
longDescription:
A fast-paced refresher course — Get up to speed with the Python skills you need for AI development. Covers core syntax, API interaction, JSON handling, async programming, and environment management.

**Goal:** Ensure you have the Python fundamentals needed to build AI applications

modules.title: Python Basics Recap — Variables, Functions, Classes
Working with APIs — The `requests` Library
JSON Handling for LLM Responses

lessons.title: Converting between Python objects and JSON

modules.title: Async Programming Basics — `asyncio` and `aiohttp`
Virtual Environments and Dependency Management

title: LLM API Keys & Interaction Reference
description: *A hands-on reference guide — one lesson per provider. Come back anytime you need a refresher on setting up or calling a specific LLM API.*
longDescription:
*A hands-on reference guide — one lesson per provider. Come back anytime you need a refresher on setting up or calling a specific LLM API.*

**Goal:** Know how to set up, authenticate, and interact with every major LLM provider

modules.title: Anthropic Claude (Lab)
OpenAI (GPT) (Lab)
Google Gemini (Lab)

lessons.title: Creating a Google AI Studio account

modules.title: Meta Llama (Lab)

lessons.title: Calling Meta Llama API with Python

modules.title: Mistral AI (Lab)
Cohere (Lab)
Ollama — Run Any LLM Locally (Lab)
Amazon Bedrock (Lab)
Azure OpenAI Service (Lab)

lessons.title: Using the openai SDK with Azure

modules.title: Hugging Face — The Open AI Platform (Lab)

lessons.title: Using OpenAI SDK with Hugging Face

modules.title: Quick Comparison & Cheat Sheet (Lab)

title: Prompt Engineering Fundamentals
description: Master the art and science of communicating with LLMs — Learn how to craft effective prompts, use system messages, apply prompting techniques, and get structured outputs reliably.
longDescription:
Master the art and science of communicating with LLMs — Learn how to craft effective prompts, use system messages, apply prompting techniques, and get structured outputs reliably.

**Goal:** Write effective prompts that produce consistent, high-quality LLM responses

modules.title: What is Prompt Engineering and Why It Matters
System Prompts vs User Prompts vs Assistant Messages
Zero-Shot, Few-Shot, and Chain-of-Thought Prompting (Lab)
Structured Output — Getting JSON Responses from LLMs (Lab)

lessons.title: Asking for JSON in the prompt

modules.title: Prompt Templates and Variables
Common Pitfalls and How to Avoid Them (Lab)

title: Introduction to AI Agents
description: Understand what AI agents are and how they work — Go beyond simple chatbots to learn the core concepts of autonomous AI agents including the ReAct pattern, agent types, and real-world use cases.
longDescription:
Understand what AI agents are and how they work — Go beyond simple chatbots to learn the core concepts of autonomous AI agents including the ReAct pattern, agent types, and real-world use cases.

**Goal:** Understand the architecture and reasoning patterns behind AI agents

modules.title: What is an AI Agent — Beyond Chatbots
Agent = LLM + Tools + Memory + Planning

lessons.title: The four pillars of an agent
The LLM as the reasoning engine

modules.title: The ReAct Pattern — Reason → Act → Observe → Repeat (Lab)
Types of Agents — Conversational, Task-Oriented, Autonomous
Agent vs Chain vs Simple LLM Call — When to Use What
Real-World Agent Examples and Use Cases

title: Tool Calling — Giving Agents Abilities
description: Teach LLMs to use tools — Learn how tool/function calling works, define tools with JSON schemas, and build agents that can search, calculate, read files, and more.
longDescription:
Teach LLMs to use tools — Learn how tool/function calling works, define tools with JSON schemas, and build agents that can search, calculate, read files, and more.

**Goal:** Build agents that can call external tools and functions to accomplish tasks

modules.title: What is Tool/Function Calling
How Tool Calling Works — The LLM Decides, Your Code Executes

lessons.title: Step 2: LLM generates a tool call

modules.title: Defining Tools with JSON Schemas (Lab)
Tool Calling with Ollama (Llama 3.1 Native Support) (Lab)

lessons.title: Setting up tool calling with Ollama

modules.title: Building Your First Tool — A Calculator Agent (Lab)
Multiple Tools — Search + Calculator + File Reader (Lab)
Handling Tool Errors Gracefully

title: Building Your First Agent with LangChain
description: Build real agents using the LangChain framework — Learn LangChain's core abstractions, create tools, build ReAct agents, and add conversation memory — all running locally with Ollama.
longDescription:
Build real agents using the LangChain framework — Learn LangChain's core abstractions, create tools, build ReAct agents, and add conversation memory — all running locally with Ollama.

**Goal:** Build functional AI agents using LangChain with Ollama

modules.title: What is LangChain — The Orchestration Layer
LangChain Core Concepts — Models, Prompts, Chains, Tools, Agents
Setting Up LangChain with Ollama (Lab)
Creating Tools with `@tool` Decorator (Lab)
Building a ReAct Agent with `create_tool_calling_agent` (Lab)
AgentExecutor — Running Agents with Verbose Tracing
Adding Conversation Memory to Agents (Lab)

title: Introduction to RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation)
description: Give LLMs access to your private data — Learn the RAG pipeline from scratch: text splitting, embeddings, vector databases, and building a complete "Chat with your documents" application.
longDescription:
Give LLMs access to your private data — Learn the RAG pipeline from scratch: text splitting, embeddings, vector databases, and building a complete "Chat with your documents" application.

**Goal:** Build a working RAG pipeline that lets users chat with their own documents

modules.title: The Problem — LLMs Don't Know Your Private Data
What is RAG — Retrieve → Augment → Generate
Text Splitting and Chunking Strategies (Lab)
Embeddings — Converting Text to Vectors (Lab)
Vector Databases — ChromaDB (Local, Free) (Lab)
Building a Simple RAG Pipeline (Lab)
RAG with LangChain — Document Loaders, Retrievers, Chains

title: Provider Abstraction — Write Once, Run Anywhere
description: Build LLM applications that aren't locked to a single provider — Learn to swap between Ollama, OpenAI, Claude, and others with minimal code changes using provider abstraction patterns.
longDescription:
Build LLM applications that aren't locked to a single provider — Learn to swap between Ollama, OpenAI, Claude, and others with minimal code changes using provider abstraction patterns.

**Goal:** Build agents that can seamlessly switch between LLM providers

modules.title: Why Provider Abstraction Matters
Using the OpenAI SDK with Ollama (Compatible API) (Lab)
Swapping Ollama → OpenAI → Anthropic Claude with One-Line Changes (Lab)
LangChain's Provider Abstraction Layer
Environment-Based Configuration (dev=Ollama, prod=Claude) (Lab)
Cost Tracking and Token Usage Monitoring

title: Beginner Capstone Project
description: Put everything together — Build a complete AI-Powered FAQ Assistant that uses RAG, tool calling, and provider abstraction, running locally on Ollama with a one-line swap to cloud APIs.
longDescription:
Put everything together — Build a complete AI-Powered FAQ Assistant that uses RAG, tool calling, and provider abstraction, running locally on Ollama with a one-line swap to cloud APIs.

**Goal:** Build a production-ready FAQ assistant that demonstrates all beginner-level skills

modules.title: Project: AI-Powered FAQ Assistant (Lab)

title: Deep Dive into Agent Architectures
description: Master the design patterns behind powerful AI agents — Learn Router, Planner, Executor, and Critic patterns, single vs multi-agent designs, communication strategies, state management, error recovery, and observability.
longDescription:
Master the design patterns behind powerful AI agents — Learn Router, Planner, Executor, and Critic patterns, single vs multi-agent designs, communication strategies, state management, error recovery, and observability.

**Goal:** Design and implement different agent architecture patterns for various use cases

modules.title: Agent Design Patterns — Router, Planner, Executor, Critic
Single-Agent vs Multi-Agent Architectures
Agent Communication Patterns — Sequential, Parallel, Hierarchical
State Management in Agentic Systems (Lab)
Error Handling and Recovery Strategies (Lab)
Agent Observability — Tracing and Debugging (Lab)

title: LangGraph — Graph-Based Agent Workflows
description: Build complex agent workflows as graphs — Learn LangGraph's node-edge-state model, conditional branching, cycles, parallel execution, and build sophisticated multi-step agent pipelines.
longDescription:
Build complex agent workflows as graphs — Learn LangGraph's node-edge-state model, conditional branching, cycles, parallel execution, and build sophisticated multi-step agent pipelines.

**Goal:** Design and implement graph-based agent workflows using LangGraph

modules.title: Why LangGraph — Limitations of Simple Agent Loops
Graph Concepts — Nodes, Edges, State, Conditional Branching
Building Your First LangGraph Workflow (Lab)
State Management with TypedDict and Pydantic (Lab)
Conditional Edges — Dynamic Routing Based on Agent Decisions (Lab)
Cycles and Loops — Agents That Iterate Until Done (Lab)
Parallel Execution — Running Multiple Nodes Simultaneously

title: Human-in-the-Loop Agents
description: Build agents that know when to ask for help — Implement interrupt patterns, approval workflows, escalation logic, and audit trails using LangGraph checkpoints.
longDescription:
Build agents that know when to ask for help — Implement interrupt patterns, approval workflows, escalation logic, and audit trails using LangGraph checkpoints.

**Goal:** Build agents with human oversight, approval gates, and escalation capabilities

modules.title: Why HITL — Trust, Safety, and Compliance
Interrupt Patterns — Pause Agent, Get Approval, Resume (Lab)
Implementing HITL with LangGraph Checkpoints (Lab)
Approval Workflows — Agent Proposes, Human Approves (Lab)
Escalation — When Agents Should Hand Off to Humans
Audit Trails and Logging Agent Decisions

title: Advanced RAG Patterns
description: Go beyond basic RAG — Master advanced chunking strategies, hybrid search, re-ranking, multi-query RAG, self-corrective retrieval, and evaluation techniques for production-quality retrieval.
longDescription:
Go beyond basic RAG — Master advanced chunking strategies, hybrid search, re-ranking, multi-query RAG, self-corrective retrieval, and evaluation techniques for production-quality retrieval.

**Goal:** Build production-grade RAG systems with high retrieval accuracy and answer quality

modules.title: Limitations of Naive RAG
Chunking Strategies — Semantic, Recursive, Parent-Child (Lab)
Hybrid Search — Combining Vector Search + Keyword Search (BM25) (Lab)
Re-Ranking — Cross-Encoder Models for Better Relevance (Lab)
Multi-Query RAG — Generating Multiple Search Queries (Lab)
Self-Corrective RAG — Agent Verifies and Retries Retrieval
RAG Evaluation — Measuring Retrieval Quality and Answer Quality

title: Multi-Agent Systems with CrewAI
description: Orchestrate teams of specialized AI agents — Learn CrewAI's role-based approach to multi-agent systems with agents, tasks, crews, and processes for collaborative AI workflows.
longDescription:
Orchestrate teams of specialized AI agents — Learn CrewAI's role-based approach to multi-agent systems with agents, tasks, crews, and processes for collaborative AI workflows.

**Goal:** Build multi-agent teams that collaborate to complete complex tasks

modules.title: What is CrewAI — Role-Based Multi-Agent Orchestration
Core Concepts — Agents, Tasks, Crews, Processes
Defining Agent Roles — Researcher, Writer, Reviewer, Coder (Lab)
Task Dependencies and Workflows (Lab)
Sequential vs Hierarchical Crew Processes (Lab)
Using CrewAI with Ollama (Lab)

title: Memory Systems for Agents
description: Give agents the ability to remember — Implement short-term, long-term, episodic, and entity memory systems using vector databases and advanced recall strategies.
longDescription:
Give agents the ability to remember — Implement short-term, long-term, episodic, and entity memory systems using vector databases and advanced recall strategies.

**Goal:** Build agents with robust memory systems that persist across sessions

modules.title: Types of Memory — Short-Term, Long-Term, Episodic, Semantic
Conversation Memory — Buffer, Summary, Sliding Window (Lab)
Persistent Memory with Vector Databases (Lab)
Entity Memory — Tracking Facts About People, Places, Things (Lab)
Memory Injection Strategies — When and How to Recall
Memory Management — Forgetting, Updating, Compressing (Lab)

title: Structured Outputs & Reliable Agent Responses
description: Make agent outputs predictable and trustworthy — Use Pydantic validation, the Instructor library, retry strategies, output parsers, and guardrails to guarantee structured, reliable LLM responses.
longDescription:
Make agent outputs predictable and trustworthy — Use Pydantic validation, the Instructor library, retry strategies, output parsers, and guardrails to guarantee structured, reliable LLM responses.

**Goal:** Build agents that produce consistent, validated, structured outputs every time

modules.title: The Reliability Problem with LLM Outputs
Pydantic Models for Structured Output Validation (Lab)
Instructor Library — Guaranteed Structured Outputs (Lab)
Retry Strategies and Self-Correction
Output Parsing with LangChain (Lab)
Guardrails — Input/Output Validation Frameworks (Lab)

title: Agent Tools — Building Complex Integrations
description: Build production-grade tools for AI agents — Design tools for databases, APIs, file systems, web scraping, and code execution, then compose them for complex real-world tasks.
longDescription:
Build production-grade tools for AI agents — Design tools for databases, APIs, file systems, web scraping, and code execution, then compose them for complex real-world tasks.

**Goal:** Build robust, production-ready tools that give agents real-world capabilities

modules.title: Tool Design Principles — Clear Descriptions, Proper Schemas
Building Database Tools — SQL Query Agents (Lab)
Building API Tools — REST and GraphQL Integrations (Lab)
File System Tools — Reading, Writing, Transforming Files (Lab)
Web Scraping Tools — Browser Automation with Playwright (Lab)
Code Execution Tools — Sandboxed Python Execution (Lab)
Tool Composition — Combining Tools for Complex Tasks (Lab)

title: Agent Evaluation & Testing
description: Test and evaluate AI agents systematically — Unit test tools, integration test workflows, use LLM-as-judge for output quality, benchmark with test datasets, and set up continuous evaluation with LangSmith.
longDescription:
Test and evaluate AI agents systematically — Unit test tools, integration test workflows, use LLM-as-judge for output quality, benchmark with test datasets, and set up continuous evaluation with LangSmith.

**Goal:** Build a comprehensive testing and evaluation pipeline for AI agents

modules.title: Why Testing Agents is Different from Testing Software
Unit Testing Tools and Individual Components (Lab)
Integration Testing Agent Workflows (Lab)
LLM-as-Judge — Using a Model to Evaluate Agent Outputs (Lab)
Benchmarking with Test Datasets (Lab)
Regression Testing — Catching Agent Behavior Changes
LangSmith for Tracing and Evaluation (Lab)

title: Intermediate Capstone Project
description: Build a complete AI-Powered Customer Support System — A multi-agent crew with RAG knowledge base, database tools, human-in-the-loop approvals, full LangGraph workflow, and comprehensive testing.
longDescription:
Build a complete AI-Powered Customer Support System — A multi-agent crew with RAG knowledge base, database tools, human-in-the-loop approvals, full LangGraph workflow, and comprehensive testing.

**Goal:** Build a production-ready multi-agent customer support system demonstrating all intermediate skills

modules.title: Project: AI-Powered Customer Support System (Lab)

title: Production Architecture for Agentic Systems
description: Design scalable, production-ready architectures for AI agents — Microservices vs monolith, event-driven design, message queues, API gateways, and horizontal scalability patterns.
longDescription:
Design scalable, production-ready architectures for AI agents — Microservices vs monolith, event-driven design, message queues, API gateways, and horizontal scalability patterns.

**Goal:** Architect agentic AI systems that scale reliably in production

modules.title: Architectural Patterns — Microservices vs Monolith for Agents
Separation of Concerns — Orchestrator, Workers, Tools, Memory

API Gateway Design for Agent Endpoints (Lab)

description: Package and deploy AI agents with Docker and Kubernetes — Best practices for AI containers, multi-stage builds, Docker Compose, DinD sandboxing, Kubernetes fundamentals, operators, and Helm charts.
longDescription:

longDescription:

longDescription:

**Goal:** Deploy and operate a production agentic AI system on AWS

longDescription:

title: Scalability, Performance & Cost Optimization
description: Make agentic systems fast, scalable, and cost-effective — Load balancing, auto-scaling, caching strategies, model routing, token optimization, batch processing, cost monitoring, and rate limiting.
longDescription:
Make agentic systems fast, scalable, and cost-effective — Load balancing, auto-scaling, caching strategies, model routing, token optimization, batch processing, cost monitoring, and rate limiting.

**Goal:** Optimize agentic systems for performance, scalability, and cost efficiency at production scale

modules.title: Load Balancing Agent Requests
Auto-Scaling Strategies — Scaling on Queue Depth, Latency, Token Usage (Lab)
Caching Strategies — Prompt Caching, Semantic Caching, Response Caching (Lab)
Model Routing — Using Cheaper Models for Simple Tasks, Expensive Models for Complex Ones (Lab)
Token Optimization — Prompt Compression, Context Pruning
Batch Processing vs Real-Time Inference
Cost Monitoring and Budgeting — Per-Agent, Per-User, Per-Tenant (Lab)
Rate Limiting and Throttling

title: Security, Compliance & Guardrails
description: Secure agentic AI systems for production — Threat modeling, prompt injection defense, tool sandboxing, PII handling, secrets management, network security, compliance, and content safety.
longDescription:
Secure agentic AI systems for production — Threat modeling, prompt injection defense, tool sandboxing, PII handling, secrets management, network security, compliance, and content safety.

**Goal:** Implement comprehensive security and compliance for production agentic AI systems

modules.title: Threat Model for Agentic AI Systems
Prompt Injection Attacks — Detection and Prevention (Lab)
Tool Call Validation and Sandboxing (Lab)
PII Detection and Redaction in Agent Flows (Lab)
Secrets Management — Azure Key Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault (Lab)
Network Security — VPCs, Private Endpoints, Firewall Rules
Audit Logging and Compliance (SOC 2, GDPR, HIPAA Considerations)
Content Safety — Input/Output Filtering (Lab)

title: Monitoring, Observability & Reliability
description: Make agentic systems observable and reliable — Logs, metrics, traces with OpenTelemetry, LLM-specific monitoring, alerting, health checks, circuit breakers, graceful degradation, and disaster recovery.
longDescription:
Make agentic systems observable and reliable — Logs, metrics, traces with OpenTelemetry, LLM-specific monitoring, alerting, health checks, circuit breakers, graceful degradation, and disaster recovery.

**Goal:** Build comprehensive observability and reliability for production agent systems

modules.title: Observability Pillars for Agents — Logs, Metrics, Traces
Distributed Tracing for Multi-Agent Systems (OpenTelemetry) (Lab)
LLM-Specific Metrics — Token Usage, Latency, Cost, Error Rates (Lab)
LangSmith / LangFuse for Agent Observability (Lab)
Alerting on Agent Failures, Hallucinations, and Anomalies
Health Checks and Circuit Breakers (Lab)
Graceful Degradation — Fallback Strategies When LLM APIs Go Down
Disaster Recovery and Backup Strategies

longDescription:

longDescription:

modules.title: Project: Production Agentic AI Platform on Cloud (Lab)
